title: Inline Scanning, Delimiters, Escapes, Code Spans, and Emphasis · Runtime Lens: Escape Entity
summary: A regex pairs the first and last asterisks, parses markup inside code, mishandles backslash escapes, loses literal delimiters, and becomes quadratic on long adversarial input. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the escape entity increment in bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Distinguish source escapes, character references, literal text, and later HTML output encoding.
Classify delimiter runs from surrounding characters, track opener and closer state, and resolve nested emphasis deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-tokenization-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-tokenization-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_inline_tokenization_2(input_units: int = 64, consumed_units: int = 64, delimiter_pairs: int = 4, literal_units: int = 21, work_units: int = 112) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if input_units < 0 or consumed_units < 0:
        return False, consumed_units, "inline-count"
    if consumed_units != input_units:
        return False, consumed_units, "unconsumed-input"
    if delimiter_pairs < 0 or literal_units < 0:
        return False, delimiter_pairs, "inline-shape"
    if work_units < input_units or work_units > input_units * 4:
        return False, work_units, "complexity-budget"
    assert consumed_units == input_units
    return True, delimiter_pairs, "bounded-inline-tokens"

Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish source escapes, character references, literal text, and later HTML output encoding.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish source escapes, character references, literal text, and later HTML output encoding.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
